behavior_summary: Provides the in-game behavior for the NPC Pyros. When examined (U8P_PYROS::look), Pyros announces his title and sets NPC info/exclude flags.
Supports an interactive 'use' sequence (dialog + conditional escalation) that can spawn a fire spell, start continuous channeling/animation, and enter complex attack/summon routines.

Contains helper routines that produce repeated directional visual/sound effects (channel), create fire sprites and projectile-like items, and attempt legal spawning with retries before hurling created items.
Behavior varies by a small local state byte and by world state (global flag 'acolyteKilled'), allowing different dialog/attack variants.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: main entry PYROS::look is triggered by the player inspecting the NPC (Event 0) and unconditionally barks a title string then suspends.
Long interactive path: PYROS::use orchestrates many barks, spawns FIRESPEL::1CEB and several PYROS helper spawns, and conditionally transitions into attack/summon sequences.
Continuous loop: U8P_PYROS::PYROS::00F3 implements a repeating/channel loop that runs while global doChannel is true; it spawns short delays and directional effect spawns between iterations.
Retry/creation loop: U8P_PYROS::PYROS::018D computes offsets and repeatedly calls Item::legal_create with bounded retries (local byte counters) until spawn succeeds, then uses Item::hurl.
Many asynchronous behaviors are implemented via spawn (nonblocking) and suspend points rather than synchronous blocking calls.
open_questions: Exact numerical IDs and semantics of many small mode/flag bytes passed to METHOD::15E7 and METHOD::091C (effects and animation variants are inferred but not confirmed).

Precise contents and side-effects of spawned helpers (e.g., PYROS::16FD, PYROS::178F, 17FE, 1914, 1883, 1EFA) referenced by use(); their high-level roles are inferred from usage but not listed in the excerpt.
Exact meaning of the local state byte comparisons (values like 0x1F and 0x20) and how those map to narrative/attack variants.
Detailed parameter semantics and return conventions of utility externals FREE::1A96 / FREE::1A85 (inferred as random/time utilities) remain uncertain.

main_function.name: U8P_PYROS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player examining (looking at) the Pyros NPC: set NPC info/exclude state, make Pyros 'bark' his title string, free the temporary string, then yield/suspend.
entry_conditions: Invoked by Event 0 (player/actor 'U8P_PYROS::look' or inspect action on the Pyros NPC).
No additional runtime gating observed in the excerpt — occurs whenever the U8P_PYROS::look event fires.

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_PYROS::look' (examine) that announces Pyros's title and sets info/exclude flags.

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::00B8
function_map.role: Utility wrapper that sets NPC info, spawns two follow-up helper spawns, and calls Item::bark with a supplied string (used by use() to present lines).

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::00F3
function_map.role: Channeling/loop routine that, while global doChannel is true, spawns short delays, plays SFX, queries facing, and repeatedly spawns directional visual/effect steps.

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::018D
function_map.role: Attack/summon helper: computes spawn coordinates, creates fire sprites, attempts Item::legal_create in a retry loop and on success calls Item::hurl; includes short barks and variant behavior based on a local state byte.

function_map.name: U8P_PYROS::use (PYROS::0413)
function_map.role: Player 'use' interaction: runs an extended dialog sequence, checks map/global flags (acolyteKilled), spawns FIRESPEL::1CEB and several PYROS subroutines, and eventually starts active attack/spawn behaviors.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine function to display a line of dialog/text from an item/NPC; used heavily for Pyros's barks.

function_map.name: Item::legal_create
function_map.role: Attempt to create an item/creature at a map position; returns success/failure and created id used for subsequent hurl.

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Apply velocity/throw to an item created by legal_create (used to launch projectiles).

internal_calls.purpose: Present dialog lines and set up short helper spawns; used repeatedly during the long 'use' interaction.
Start continuous channeling/animation (inferred: use() spawns continuous visual behavior via 00F3).
Spawn attack/summon sequences after dialog/effects; 018D handles coordinate computation, sprite creation, legal_create retry and hurl.
Spawn a short-lived helper/visual routine immediately after barking (exact role unclear from excerpt).
Spawn another short-lived helper/visual routine; paired with 16FD following a bark.

external_calls.purpose: Display the static title string 'Pyros, Lord of Flame ' to the examining player/process.
Create a short nonblocking delay between iterations of the channeling loop.
Produce directional visual/effect steps based on the NPC's facing; called repeatedly per loop iteration.
Obtain base coordinates to place a fire sprite and to attempt legal_create spawn positions.
Create a fire visual at computed coordinates as part of the summon/attack spectacle.
Attempt to create a projectile/creature at adjusted coordinates; result is tested and retried if needed.
Launch the successfully created item as a projectile with computed velocities.
Compute random or timed offsets used to perturb spawn coordinates and delays (inferred utility role).
Trigger a fire spell routine as part of the use() escalation.

reads.name: doChannel
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether the channeling loop (U8P_PYROS::PYROS::00F3) continues running; a global toggle for continuous visual/attack channeling.

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: Read during U8P_PYROS::PYROS::use to select dialog/behavior variants (influences which attack/spawn sequences run or how many acolytes/variants are produced).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the in-game behavior for the NPC Pyros. When examined (U8P_PYROS::look), Pyros announces his title and sets NPC info/exclude flags.
